query: Can you create a new Python CLI for me? I want to parse markdown files and extract code blocks. Project should be called 'md-codeblock-extractor' under my personal account 'sm-tools'.
I need a new Python project. Going to build a small REST API for serving model predictions. Project name 'inference-gateway', owner 'ml-platform-co'.
Can you create a UV project? I'm experimenting with embedding databases — want it called 'pgvector-playground', owner 'alice-research'.
Start a new python project for me, calling it 'data-cleaner'. Should be a CLI tool for normalizing CSV files.
Scaffold a Python package that wraps the OpenAI API with retry logic — call it 'oai-retry-wrapper', personal account.
I want to build a Python tool — can you set up a fresh project? It's going to be a CLI for converting between time zones with DST handling. Name: 'tz-shifter'.
Make me a new Python CLI tool, working name 'feed-parser'. It'll pull RSS feeds and dedupe entries.
I need to start a Python project to play around with web scraping — going to scrape some product pages for price tracking. Owner is 'datatools-co', repo 'price-watcher'.
Help me create a new Python project for processing CSV files into parquet. Should run as a CLI, name 'csv2parquet', under my org 'analytics-team'.
ok let's spin up a new python project — I want to build a small thing that monitors my Hue lights via the local bridge API. Personal account, name 'hue-watcher'.
Add a new Python module to my existing project for handling Redis caching. The project is in ~/projects/web-api and I want the module at app/cache.py.
Convert this Python script into a proper class structure with type hints. Currently it's all functions in main.py.
Fix the bug in my Python project's auth module — JWT validation is rejecting tokens that should be valid. The token contains an aud claim with a list of audiences.
What's the difference between Poetry and uv for Python dependency management? I'm trying to decide which to standardize on for our team.
Show me how to write a Python decorator that times function calls and emits the duration to stdout in microseconds.
Help me debug why my Python tests are failing. The test passes individually but fails when the whole pytest suite runs — looks like state is leaking between tests.
I'm building a Rust CLI, can you scaffold a Cargo project? It's going to be a TUI for monitoring kubernetes pods. Name: 'pod-watch'.
Create a new Node.js project for me. Going to build a discord bot, name 'mod-helper', using TypeScript and bun.
Optimize this Python function for memory usage. It currently loads the whole CSV into memory; should stream row by row.
Run pytest in my Python project and tell me which tests are failing. Project is at ~/projects/analytics-cli.
